decision_point: In an adult with chronic, recalcitrant lateral epicondylitis, is surgical debridement or structured conservative therapy preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with chronic, recalcitrant lateral epicondylitis, is surgical debridement or structured conservative therapy preferred?
Options:
  1. Structured conservative therapy
  2. Surgical debridement
label: settled
label_rationale: Lateral epicondylitis is largely self-limiting; structured conservative therapy is first-line, with long-term outcomes little different from natural history. Debridement (Nirschl) is reserved for truly recalcitrant cases. Settled conservative-first standard (MD adjudication 2026-06-27; reproducibly converged at N=3).
